description_long: This is a realistic crab with a robust, rounded carapace featuring a mottled pattern of gray, brown, and orange hues. The crab has two prominent claws (chelae), with one noticeably larger and more orange-tinted than the other, and four pairs of segmented walking legs extending from the sides. Its carapace is textured and slightly rough, with visible grooves and small bumps, and the eyes are set on short stalks at the front. The legs and claws are jointed, with the tips of the claws and some leg segments showing a darker, almost black coloration. The underside and inner surfaces of the limbs are lighter and less textured. The overall appearance suggests a hard, calcified exoskeleton typical of marine crabs, with subtle color transitions and naturalistic surface details.
description: A lifelike crab with a mottled gray and orange carapace, two large claws, and eight segmented legs, featuring a hard, textured shell and eyes on short stalks.
description_view_0: Front view showing both claws extended forward, the carapace's detailed texture, and the eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the larger orange claw and the segmentation of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace, the attachment points of the legs, and the symmetry of the limbs.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the smaller claw and the curvature of the legs.
